Understand the isolation context.. Before fixing a warning, determine:
The main actor (@MainActor)
A specific actor instance (actor MyActor { ... })
Nonisolated context (any Task, detached task, or nonisolated function)
Use @MainActor for types and functions that must run on the main thread:
Use nonisolated for properties and methods that do not touch actor state:
Use when you need to run multiple async operations and collect results:
Use only when you need to launch work that outlives the current scope:

Identify the isolation domains.. What actor is the source? What actor
Apply the correct pattern:
Value type with all Sendable fields? It is already Sendable.
Reference type with immutable state? Mark final class: Sendable.
Reference type with mutable state? Convert to actor.
Closure captures mutable state? Extract the needed values into let
Need to call @MainActor from background? Use await or Task { @MainActor in }.

Check existing models.. Grep the project's Core/Models/ directory
Must be final class.. The @Model macro requires reference type semantics
All stored properties are persisted. unless marked @Transient. There is
Default values are required. for properties not set in init. SwiftData
uses them during migration when adding new properties.
Relationships use array syntax for to-many.. var claims: [Claim] = []
Delete rules matter.. Always specify a delete rule for relationships:
.cascade: Delete related objects (parent owns children)
.nullify: Set relationship to nil (default, safe)
.deny: Prevent deletion if related objects exist
.noAction: Do nothing (dangerous, can orphan objects)
Use separate configurations when some data should sync to CloudKit and
Adding a new property with a default value
Removing a property
Renaming a property (with @Attribute(originalName:))
All properties must have default values.. CloudKit objects arrive
No unique constraints.. CloudKit does not support uniqueness enforcement
Relationships must be optional.. Related objects may not have synced yet.
Use @Attribute(.externalStorage) for large data:

Use ContentUnavailableView for empty states instead of custom empty views:
headerSection (good) vs topPart (bad)
ConnectionRow (good) vs ListItem (bad)
[ ] Every interactive element has an accessibilityLabel
[ ] Custom controls have accessibilityAddTraits
[ ] Decorative images use accessibilityHidden(true)
[ ] Lists use accessibilityElement(children: .combine) on rows
[ ] All text uses system fonts for Dynamic Type scaling
[ ] Color is never the only means of conveying information
[ ] Custom actions are provided for complex gestures
